summary: The algorithmic half of Open Problem 5 is settled: Anastos, Lamaison, Steiner, and Szabó (arXiv:1911.01954) proved that deciding whether a given digraph is majority 2-colorable is NP-complete (Theorem 9), ruling out a polynomial-time recognition algorithm unless P=NP. The structural-characterisation half of the question remains open, as no combinatorial characterisation of majority 2-colorable digraphs is known.

since_posted.title: Majority Colorings of Sparse Digraphs
since_posted.authors: Michael Anastos, Ander Lamaison, Raphael Steiner, Tibor Szabó
since_posted.claim: Proves that deciding whether a digraph is majority 2-colorable is NP-complete (Theorem 9), answering the polynomial-time algorithm part of Open Problem 5 negatively; the structural characterisation question remains open.

internal_refs_verified.note: Abstract explicitly states 'we answer an open question posed by Kreutzer et al. negatively, by showing that deciding whether a given digraph is majority 2-colorable is NP-complete'; confirms Theorem 9 claim from internal refs.

search_queries: majority coloring digraph characterization recognition complexity 2024 2025
notes: Open Problem 5 asks two things: (1) a characterisation of majority 2-colorable digraphs, or (2) a polynomial-time algorithm. The NP-completeness result in 1911.01954 definitively closes question (2). Question (1) remains open; a 2025 paper (Acta Math. Appl. Sinica) on majority coloring of digraphs was found but is behind a paywall and could not be verified. A 2024 arXiv paper (2406.04189) on countable DAGs addresses a different variant and does not resolve the finite characterisation question.
